rationale: The citing paper uses Chen and Yang (2007) only as support for a broad background claim that aggregate data are often more representative, available, and cost-effective than household panel data. There is no substantive discussion of the cited paper's data-augmentation approach, dynamic disaggregate estimation, or specific results in the model development, identification, estimation, or interpretation sections. The paper's methodological positioning is mainly built on BLP and Bruno and Vilcassim, so this citation functions as a passing literature support reference.
